summary: Iteration 4 — Live LLM (Claude Sonnet 4.5 via Emergent universal key) integration tested end-to-end. Backend POST /api/chat exercised with a free-form question for all 3 projects and 4 error/regression cases — 7/7 pytest assertions PASSED in 16.1s. Frontend Playwright smoke covered: Live LLM toggle pill, Claude Sonnet 4.5 sub-text + greeting badge, real /api/chat round-trip with typing indicator + Live badge + confidence + cited snippet, scripted toggle round-trip, project switch (P1->P2) reset + greeting refresh, regression of /dashboard /projects /workflow /valuation /reports. Zero console or page errors observed. Fallback code path 'Live LLM unavailable' verified present in /app/frontend/src/pages/Chatbot.jsx line 87.
tests_executed: POST /api/chat MMRDA-2026-0142 'outstanding mortgage' -> 200, model=claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929, citation chunk starts p1-
POST /api/chat MMRDA-2026-0098 'is title clear?' -> 200, citation chunk starts p2-
POST /api/chat MMRDA-2026-0117 'why disputed?' -> 200, citation chunk starts p3-
Chatbot header shows 'Live LLM' toggle pill + 'Claude Sonnet 4.5' sub-text
Greeting message renders with 'Live · Claude Sonnet 4.5' badge
Free-form question triggers /api/chat POST -> typing indicator 'Claude Sonnet 4.5 reasoning over Yavi.ai corpus…' -> assistant message with Live badge + confidence + Cited snippet box
Toggle off Live -> pill text becomes 'Scripted'; subsequent message renders with 'Scripted' badge
Toggle back to Live -> pill returns to 'Live LLM'
Project switcher P1->P2 -> chatbot conversation reset + greeting now mentions 'Mumbai Trans Harbour Link' and 'MMRDA-2026-0098'
Regression nav: /dashboard /projects /workflow /valuation /reports all render without errors
Code-path check: 'Live LLM unavailable' fallback string present at Chatbot.jsx:87
critical_code_review_comments: server.py /api/chat properly validates project_id (400) and empty message (400), and returns 502 on Claude failure with detail — clean error handling.
_parse_llm_json() in server.py is robustly defensive: strips ```json fences, falls back to first {...} block, and gracefully returns {} on JSONDecodeError. Good.
model field is hard-coded to 'claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929' in the response — matches review spec exactly.
session_id is generated per request as 'mmrda-{project_id}-{8hex}' — fine for stateless RAG calls; if conversation memory is later required, this should be persisted client-side and reused.
Citation field truncation (doc 200, chunk 80, line 400) is sensible defensive sizing.

Chatbot.jsx: useEffect dependency array includes liveMode (line 55) which means toggling resets the entire conversation. This is acceptable per spec ('greeting refreshes') but may surprise users mid-conversation; consider preserving prior messages and only refreshing the greeting badge in a future iteration.
Chatbot.jsx fallback path on live failure (lines 84-93) appends a system note then continues to scripted — exactly as spec'd. Good.

Backend logger is initialised AFTER it is referenced inside chat_with_yavi (logger defined at module bottom line 194 but used at line 146). Python evaluates the name at call-time so this works, but it is fragile — recommend moving the logging.basicConfig + logger = ... block above the route handlers.
EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is read from env at request time (good) and returns 503 if missing (good).
No Pydantic validation on max message length — could let users submit very large prompts. Consider a soft cap (e.g. 2000 chars) to bound Claude cost.
test_credentials: Mock auth — pre-filled 'officer' / 'mmrda@2026', role 'Legal Officer'. EMERGENT_LLM_KEY already set in /app/backend/.env.
seed_data_creation: None — PROJECT_CONTEXT corpus is static in /app/backend/project_context.py.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 4 fully passes. /api/chat is live and grounded per-project (citation chunks always carry the correct p1/p2/p3 prefix — verified for all 3 projects with real Claude calls). Frontend Live/Scripted toggle, typing indicator copy, badges, confidence, cited snippet, project-switch reset, and fallback code path are all wired correctly. One minor non-blocking nit: backend logger is initialised AFTER it is referenced (works but fragile). Consider moving logger init to top of server.py.
rca of the issue: No issues found in this iteration.
